amendments.body: `update_task_run` still accepts the wrong finalized runless attempt when the caller later supplies a `run_id`. In `src/adapters/fs.rs:3013-3017`, the no-exact-match path falls back to `unique_terminal_replay_match`, and that helper (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2756-2788`) never checks `started_at` for runless terminal rows. A replay of a legacy finalized attempt with the wrong `started_at` will therefore backfill `run_id` onto that terminal row instead of rejecting it, even though Amendment 2 required `started_at` to disambiguate runless-to-named backfills. Add the same `started_at` guard for terminal fallback that you added for open matches, and cover it with a regression test.
amendments.rationale: This is a remaining correctness bug in the lineage repair path: the wrong historical attempt can still be mutated during terminal replay.

amendments.body: The `plan_hash` auto-population fix is still incomplete for repair/replay flows. The snapshot hash is only consulted when appending a brand-new start row (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2932-2943`), but both existing-row branches explicitly skip snapshot backfill (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2896-2907` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:3215-3237`). That means a legacy or partial-write `TaskRunEntry` with `plan_hash: None` will stay unlabeled forever on start replay or completion replay, even when the milestone snapshot already held the canonical plan hash for that attempt. The accepted Issue 3 fix called out `update_task_run` as well, so the repair paths still need a safe snapshot-based backfill rule and tests for existing-row recovery.
amendments.rationale: This leaves lineage data incomplete in exactly the legacy/recovery scenarios this task is supposed to repair.

amendments.body: The journal-detail drift fix is not complete yet. `StartJournalDetails` and `CompletionJournalDetails` are now shared model structs (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:353-394`), but the adapter still hand-merges only the current optional fields in `src/adapters/fs.rs:2284-2305` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:2322-2354`. If a new field is added to the shared structs later, the code will still compile and the new round-trip tests will still pass, but `append_event_if_missing` will silently drop that field during journal repair because `merge` never copies it. Add a merge-coverage test or refactor the merge logic so future field additions fail loudly instead of truncating repaired events.
amendments.rationale: This is a significant maintainability risk and means Acceptance Criterion 1 is not actually enforced for future field additions.

payload.summary: Reviewed the milestone lineage changes in `src/adapters/fs.rs`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs`, and `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs`, and ran `cargo test --lib contexts::milestone_record` successfully. The open runless-to-named path is fixed, but I found one remaining terminal replay correctness bug, one incomplete `plan_hash` recovery path for legacy rows, and one remaining future-drift risk in the journal repair merge logic.
